name: 英兰妮
current_positions: Minister in the Prime Minister's Office
Member of the Parliament of Singapore

work_experience.position: Lawyer; made partner within 5 years; appointed Senior Counsel in 2003
Member of Parliament (Tanjong Pagar GRC 2001-2025, Pasir Ris-Changi GRC since 2025)

work_experience.organization: Ministry of Law / Ministry of Education
work_experience.position: Senior Minister of State for Law and Education
Minister in the Prime Minister's Office

person_relationships.description: Succeeded Shanmugam's Second Minister for Law role; both are Senior Counsel and Tamil Indian lawyers in PAP
Both are Senior Counsel who served in law-related ministerial portfolios
Father; former Deputy Commissioner of Police and President of the Singapore National Olympic Council

family_members.name: A.T. Rajah
family_members.industry_or_organization: Former Deputy Commissioner of Police; President of Singapore National Olympic Council

political_stances.stance_content: Led the ASPIRE report which resulted in the establishment of SkillsFuture Singapore, a national movement for lifelong learning and career guidance aligned with economic demand.

Co-chaired the Committees on Family Justice, formation of the Singapore International Commercial Court, and the Penal Code Review Committee whose recommendations led to major amendments including greater protections against sexual offences.
Oversees the National Population and Talent Division in PMO, assisting Senior Minister Teo Chee Hean on population matters.

major_achievements.achievement: Led the ASPIRE report resulting in the establishment of SkillsFuture Singapore
Fourth woman to become a full Minister in Singapore's history
Co-chaired formation of the Singapore International Commercial Court

biography_summary: Indranee Thurai Rajah is a Singaporean politician and lawyer who has served as Minister in the Prime Minister's Office and Second Minister for Finance since 2018, Second Minister for National Development and Leader of the House since 2020. The daughter of former Deputy Commissioner of Police A.T. Rajah, she is of Tamil Hindu and Chinese Anglican heritage. She was a practising lawyer and Senior Counsel before entering politics. She became the fourth woman to become a full Minister in Singapore. She co-chaired the committees that established the Singapore International Commercial Court and led the ASPIRE report creating SkillsFuture Singapore. She has been MP for Tanjong Pagar GRC (2001-2025) and Pasir Ris-Changi GRC (since 2025).

collection_meta.notes: Enriched with family members (A.T. Rajah), political stances on education/law reform/population, major achievements including SkillsFuture and SICC, person relationships from Wikipedia and web search
